selection: 按 4–7 岁 阅读者的常用词频与中小学词汇标记筛选

words.translation: n. 例证, 插图

words.translation: n. 一点点, 少许, 一会儿, 短时间；a. 小的, 很少的, 幼小的, 琐碎的, 短暂的, 矮小的

words.translation: n. 小麦

words.translation: n. 猫, 恶妇；vi. 呕吐

words.translation: n. 切口, 割伤, 降低, 切, 割, 砍, 削, 伤口, 削减, 缩短, 删节, 通路；a. 经切割的, 缩减的

words.translation: n. 植物, 作物, 工厂, 树枝, 生长, 设施, 成套设备；vt. 种植, 栽培, 播种, 培养, 安置, 殖民于, 使位于

words.translation: adv. 慢慢地, 迟缓地
words.definition: r. without speed (`slow' is sometimes used informally for `slowly')

words.translation: n. 微风, 煤屑, 轻而易举的事；vi. 吹微风, 逃走
words.definition: n. a slight wind (usually refreshing)

words.translation: a. 远的, 疏远的
words.definition: a. separated in space or coming from or going to a distance

words.translation: n. 制造, (手工)制造业, 制作, 形成, 发展, 要素, 内在因素, 赚头, 制造物
words.definition: n. (usually plural) the components needed for making or doing something

words.translation: n. 食谱, 处方, 秘诀
words.definition: n. directions for making something

words.translation: n. 环境, 背景, 布景, 镶嵌, 调整, 沉落, 一副餐具
words.definition: n. the context and environment in which something is set

words.translation: n. 威廉姆斯（男子名）
words.definition: n. United States country singer and songwriter (1923-1953)

words.translation: n. 约克郡；约克王朝
words.definition: n. the English royal house (a branch of the Plantagenet line) that reigned from 1461 to 1485; its emblem was a white rose

words.translation: n. 英语；a. 英文的, 英国人的
words.definition: n. an Indo-European language belonging to the West Germanic branch; the official language of Britain and the United States and most of the commonwealth countrie

words.translation: prep. 向, 对于, 为了；a. 即将来临的, 进行中的
words.definition: prep. Alt. of Towards

words.translation: n. 生面团
words.definition: n. a flour mixture stiff enough to knead or roll

words.translation: a. 拔出的；draw的过去分词
words.definition: s. having the curtains or draperies closed or pulled shut

words.translation: vi. 逐渐消失, 逐渐减少
a. 已知的, 有名的；know的过去分词
words.definition: a. apprehended with certainty

words.translation: n. 厂主, 蛾；[机] 铣床
words.definition: n. United States bandleader of a popular big band (1909-1944)
